ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Xacutti Lamb. Closest defensible match is lamb curry (FOODON_03315595). Xacutti is a specific Goan preparation with coconut and whole spices not individually coded in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_description: Lamb meat dish — cooked, spiced, with coconut
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated Xacutti code. A0CKP (cooked lamb meat dish) is the closest defensible anchor. Coconut and spice matrix are characteristic of this preparation but not separately coded at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Xacutti Lamb or Goan lamb curry as a clinical food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Xacutti Lamb is approximately 250 g, comprising a generous ladle of lamb pieces in thick coconut-spice gravy, usually accompanied by rice or bread.

nutrient_source.title: Xacutti Lamb — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Xacutti Lamb (cr_id G00010854). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry and is estimated from USDA FoodData Central lamb curry analogues.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on lamb curry with coconut and mixed spices
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Xacutti Lamb in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous meat-and-coconut curry entries. Lamb and coconut are low-GI ingredients; the spice matrix (turmeric, cinnamon, cloves) does not raise GI. Carbohydrate content is low at 6.29 g/100g. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (35 × 6.29 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 5.5 g, confirming low GL. Estimation is conservative and defensible.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 308 mg/100g is in the borderline range (200–400 mg); potassium at 254 mg/100g provides partial offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation to bring sodium below 200 mg/100g, and limit portion to 150–200 g; the potassium-to-sodium ratio is favourable but does not fully neutralise the sodium load at standard restaurant portions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (6.29 g/100g) and low sugar (1.57 g/100g) with moderate protein (8.19 g/100g) supporting glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Xacutti Lamb is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low carbohydrate and sugar content; pair with a high-fibre side such as salad or dal and monitor portion size as coconut adds caloric density.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from lamb and coconut (estimated ~3.2 g/100g) and cholesterol at 29 mg/100g; sodium at 308 mg/100g adds cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Xacutti Lamb to occasional consumption, request reduced coconut and salt in preparation, and pair with vegetables to offset the saturated fat and sodium contribution.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb is red meat rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), a primary pro-inflammatory disqualifier for RA; nightshade ingredients (dried red chillies, tomato) may amplify joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Xacutti Lamb is not recommended for rheumatoid arthritis due to the arachidonic acid content of lamb and the presence of nightshade ingredients; substituting lamb with chicken or fish and retaining the turmeric and cinnamon spice base is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Xacutti Lamb is a robust Goan-origin curry made from lamb slow-cooked in a richly spiced coconut masala. The dish originates from the coastal state of Goa in western India, where Portuguese culinary influence blended with local Konkani traditions to produce intensely aromatic, coconut-forward curries. The masala is built from pan-roasted whole spices including dried red chillies, coriander seeds, cumin, cinnamon, cloves, black pepper, and poppy seeds, which are ground with freshly grated or pan-roasted coconut to form a thick, fragrant paste. Lamb pieces are marinated and then simmered in this paste with onion, garlic, ginger, tomato, and tamarind until the meat is tender and the gravy is deeply flavoured. The texture is thick and coating, with a complex heat that builds gradually. The taste profile is simultaneously earthy, spicy, slightly tangy from tamarind, and rich from coconut fat. Per 100 g, Xacutti Lamb provides 117 kcal, 8.19 g protein, 6.39 g fat, and 6.29 g carbohydrate, making it a moderate-energy, protein-rich dish with low carbohydrate content. Sodium is 308 mg/100g, placing it in the borderline range for hypertension. The dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low sugar (1.57 g/100g) and low carbohydrate load. It is not recommended for rheumatoid arthritis due to the arachidonic acid content of red meat. In Singapore, Xacutti Lamb appears on Indian restaurant menus and is commonly served with steamed rice, naan, or appam.
common_user_queries: Is Xacutti Lamb good for diabetes?
Xacutti Lamb vs regular lamb curry
Can I eat Xacutti Lamb with hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010854, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central lamb curry analogues and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, clearly documented. All other fields are constructed from defensible sources.
notes: Saturated fat (3.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central lamb curry analogues (USDA FDC ID 174972, cooked lamb shoulder with spices) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Goan lamb preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. Halal status is marked false as Xacutti is a Goan dish of Portuguese-Hindu origin and is not universally certified halal; individual restaurant certification should be verified. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate — no direct GI measurement for Xacutti Lamb exists in the University of Sydney GI Database.
